title: EscalationContractAuditCompleted payload
description: Emitted by the chain orchestrator-tier-escalation-contract retrospective to record that the orchestrator-tier escalation contract (docs/ORCHESTRATOR_ESCALATION.md design + EscalationProposed event + escalation_thresholds config table + escalation_threshold_list/set/propose admin actions + the harness-agnostic clients/escalation Python library + the integration proof) has been verified against the chain's completion-condition items (a)-(f). Same Findings array shape as ArchitectureAuditCompleted and the other *AuditCompleted events. Self-hosting check: the contract's own EscalationProposed write-side ledger is the same ledger this closing audit lands through, exercised via go/cmd/orchestrator-escalation-audit-emit.

audit_doc.description: Pointer to the audit source-doc the findings were keyed against — docs/ORCHESTRATOR_ESCALATION.md (T1 design) and the chain's completion_condition.

summary.description: One- or two-paragraph executive summary of the audit outcome — what landed, what was deferred, which downstream driver-swap work becomes forgeable now that the contract has shipped.

recommended_next_phase.description: Free-text recommendation for the next phase of work (e.g. 'Build the cheap-orchestrator harness loop that consumes this contract'). Optional.

findings.description: One entry per completion-condition item / audit checklist item. Severity uses the audit-convention enum (low / medium / high / critical); use n/a for informational findings.

item.description: Audit checklist item identifier and short description.

status.description: pass = criterion satisfied; fail = criterion not satisfied (must file follow-on); partial = satisfied with documented gap; deferred = explicitly out of scope for this chain.

evidence.description: Concrete evidence supporting status — SQL query result, test name, commit SHA, doc pointer, etc.

severity.description: Severity if status is fail/partial. n/a for informational rows.
